see you again! Now we have 3 night meetings on top of our daily 7 am
meeting! Join us Monday and Wednesday nights for reading from the Big
Book, 12 Steps and 12 Traditions, Grapevine and 4 minute shares on the
topic/reading of the night. It's the same format as our daily 7 am
meeting. On Friday join us for an open meeting, where alcoholics and
friends and family are invited to hear one of our many amazing
speakers. All of our speakers from Sunday morning at 7am and Friday

name: Big Chunks of Truth (IN-PERSON)
notes:
Big Chunks of Truth welcomes every person of any race, ancestry, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, educational background, national origin, or religion. The only requirement to attend our meeting is a desire to stop drinking. We have a rotating format that goes: 1st Tuesday of the month is a Step Discussion (12&12), 2nd = Big Book, 3rd = Tradition, 4th = Topic. If there’s a 5th Tuesday in a month, we discuss another Step. If we have a medallion or group anniversary, then we might do an open meeting format.

If we have a medallion then we do an open meeting format.

see you again! Now we have 3 night meetings in addition to our daily 7 am

name: Primetime (ONLINE MEETING)
notes:
PRIMETIME TORONTO
ONLINE
Friday 8pm EST 5pm PST

OPEN/CLOSED -

The purpose of the Primetime AA meeting is to talk about the reason to come to Alcoholics Anonymous; to expose alcoholism not just as a word, but as a living mind-powered disease; how the disease appears and functions in our lives today in order to deepen our awareness of what we are up against. Every Friday night at 8pm EST 5pm PST
A speaker shares for 30 minutes, then we open the floor for sharing. The meeting is 1:15 mins in length.

Meeting ID: 93964606710
Passcode: primetime

Primetime Toronto is a sapling group taken from the roots of the great oak that is www.primetimeisnow.com on the Pacific Coast started by the late great Bob Anderson.

We are looking for more local GTA members to join us - this is a perfect meeting for people with busy lives who want to attend regular meetings and do service but have difficulty travelling to a physical location! We hold our business meetings the first Friday of the month.

Open meetings are available to anyone interested in Alcoholics Anonymous’ program of recovery from alcoholism. Non-alcoholics may attend open meetings as observers.
